observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to –
• direct the Municipal Accountant to –
a) prepare necessary adjusting entries for (i) reclassification of small
tangible items with estimated useful life of more than one year and with
amount valued below ₱15,000.00 to respective expense accounts, and
start recording similar purchases as inventories upon acquisition and
as expense upon issuance; and (ii) donations to the municipality upon
receiving documents relative therefrom; and
b) immediately update PPELCs.
• instruct the GSO to a) follow-up submission of donation documents,
particularly the deed of donation, and submit the same to the accounting
office for proper recording in the books; and b) prepare, maintain, and
update Property Cards for all PPEs.

We also reiterated prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed to –
• instruct the Inventory Committee to (i) conduct annual physical count of
all PPE and upon completion of the count, reconcile the result of actual
count with the existing inventory records of the accounting office and
GSO; (ii) consequently prepare the RPCPPE in prescribed format and
submit the same to the Office of the Auditor on or before January 31 of
the following year; (iii) tag all PPEs with property stickers for easy
identification; and (iv) consider conducting one-time cleansing of PPE
accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006; and
• instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate disposal of
unserviceable assets pursuant to Section 79 of PD 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to –
• ensure the completeness and propriety of the documentation for
Accounts Payable recorded at year-end; and
• revert the accounts payable not covered by perfected contracts on record
and funded out of General Fund and Special Education Fund, which have
been outstanding for two years or more, to unappropriated surplus after
verifying that there is no actual claim filed pertinent thereto whether
administrative or judicial.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the –
a) concerned contractors to provide documentation that would fully substantiate
the actual scope of work performed commensurate to the amount billed to the
Municipality for the provision of dump trucks totaling ₱22,064,640.00 for
Phases I and II and spoil sites for the desilting projects amounting to
₱1,134,980.00, for further evaluation of the Audit Team otherwise, the same
shall be refunded;
b) Municipal Planning and Development Office, in coordination with Municipal
Engineering Office, Municipal Budget Office and Municipal Accounting
Office to consider performing cost-benefit analysis in order to determine the
development projects that would truly provide long-term and optimum benefit
to its constituents; and
c) Municipal Engineering Office refund the total amount of ₱1,720,000.00
representing provision of service vehicle included in the program of works in
case of failure to present valid legal basis for such expenditure and to ensure
that the program of works do not include special items that would
unnecessarily increase the project costs pursuant to COA Circular 2012-003.

We recommended and Management agreed to -
a.) ensure that the Municipality continuously implement programs, projects and
activities that will aid and protect its constituents from the adverse effects of
pandemic;
b.) (i) advise the BAC to comply with the posting requirements of procurement
related to COVID-19 as requisite by GPPB Circular No. 02-2021 dated March
25, 2021 and RA No. 11525 or the Vaccination Program Act of 2021; and (ii)
thru the Accounting Department, ascertain that transactions are supported
with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) and (7) of PD
1445; and
c.) direct the Bids and Awards Committee utilize the Philippine Drug Price Index
(DPRI) as a guide in ensuring the reasonableness of the prices of essential
medicines being procured by the Municipality.

We recommended that :
a) Management require the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Bids
and Awards Committee to ensure that procurement of infrastructure
projects/civil works are in accordance with pertinent sections of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA 9184 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445; and
b) the Engineering Department stop including provision of service vehicle to
engineers in the preparation of program of works and bill of materials, that would
unnecessarily increase the project costs pursuant to COA Circular 2012-003.

We recommended and Management agreed to -
a. instruct the Municipal Assessor to complete the tax mapping of the three
remaining barangays so that proper valuation of real properties be made;
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain subsidiary
ledgers (SLs) to support of the RPT and SET accounts in order to have proper
reconciliation with the MTO regularly; (ii) set up the amount of RPT and SET
Receivables at the beginning of the year based on Real Property Tax Account
Register/Taxpayer’s index card; and (iii) make necessary adjustment to reflect
the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivable; and
c. require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the accounting office copy of (i)
duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year, and not just summary of collectibles for the year; and (ii)
Distribution of Barangay Share on RPT Collected by Provincial Treasurer’s
Office to correctly identify the corresponding penalties.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to instruct the –
(a) Bids and Awards Committee to ensure faithful compliance with RA 9184 and
its IRR and other pertinent laws and regulations pertaining to hiring of
consultants, as applicable; and
(b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that Accomplishment Reports of the
consultants include specific activities performed within the billing period
prior to processing their payments.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
through the ICO-Human Resources Management Officer to (a) review its existing
organizational structure and staffing pattern in order to determine the appropriate
human resource complement required for the effective discharge of duties and
functions of the LGU; and (b) consider for appointment the existing qualified Job
Order personnel to the vacant positions, subject to civil service laws, rules, and
regulations, as well as the authorized Personnel Services limitations. We also
recommended that the LGU adhere to the policies and guidelines in hiring of
personnel under contract of service and job orders as provided in CSC Resolution
No. 020790.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the –
(a) MDRRMO to ensure optimal utilization of the LDRRMF through the full
implementation of the projects and activities in accordance with Republic Act
No. 10121 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce risks;
(b) Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the disbursements to be charged
under the LDRRMF are aligned with the goals and objectives of the fund
pursuant to Item 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Section 21 of RA No.
10121 prior to certifying in the CAFOA; and
(c) Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to effect settlement of
the deficiencies noted amounting to ₱6,797,036.50 and to see to it that all
disbursements are charged properly against the corresponding fund provided
by laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management agreed that a) the duly authorized
representative/s of the various sectors to the newly reconstituted GFPS be identified
for proper delineation of duties and responsibilities; and b) the GFPS (i) submit the
GPB to the Audit Team within the prescribed period for proper evaluation thereof;
and (ii) ensure that the PPAs are in alignment with the GAD objectives of truly
addressing gender issues towards gender equality and women empowerment.

We recommended and Management agreed that they remain compliant with
laws, accounting, budgeting and auditing rules and regulations and the Local Chief
Executive continuously take the necessary actions on the settlement of the
disallowances.
We recommended that The Municipality follow the procurements set by the DOH and the NTF in the procurement of COVID-19 Vaccines and its ancillary supplies and services.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU's procurements made through Negotiated Procurement (Emergency Cases) were resorted during the height of the pandemic to ensure the steady supply of medicines and ancillary supplies necessary to address the pressing need.
Management added that due diligence was observed to ensure that the prices availed were not disadvantageous to the government.
It was assured that the LGU will refrain from adopting this measure as much as possible.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Treasurer instruct the –
(i) Accountable Officers to –
(a) maintain separate Cashbooks for each kind of cash advances, and regularly reconcile their accountabilities with the accounting records; and
(b) coordinate with the recipients, taking into consideration the existing constraints brought about by the pandemic, to facilitate the liquidation of the cash advances within the prescribed period depending upon the nature and purpose of the particular cash advance or as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served.
observations.managementAction:
Cashbooks were issued by the Municipal Treasurer to each custodian. Only Accountable Officers from MTO and Petty Cash Fund have maintained and updated cashbook.
The Accounting Office coordinated with the Accountable Officers to ensure the timely liquidation of their accounts.
observations.reason: Various cash advances were not timely liquidated on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Petty Cash Fund Custodian to replenish the PCF as soon as the disbursement reached 75% of the fund or as the need arises, to ensure availability/sufficiency of funds for petty operating expenses at any given time.
observations.managementAction: Petty Cash Custodians were reminded to prepare their Petty Cash Vouchers and supporting documents as soon as possible and to have them verified by the Accounting Office prior to replenishment to ensure that as they reach their replenishment point, the process will be swiftly facilitated.
observations.reason: Some Petty Cash Custodians did not replenish fund as they reach their replenishment point.

observations.recommendation:
In this regard, we recommended and Management agreed to –
a. strictly implement projects in accordance with the priority programs and projects identified and in accordance with relevant guidelines such as LBC No. 134 accordingly, and to ensure that these are charged to the proper funds provided therefor; and
b. direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular 2012-001 and other relevant rules and regulations, before making any payment.
observations.managementAction: Partially Implemented.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the –
a) BAC, BAC Secretariat and BAC TWG to –
i. justify in writing, the reason for awarding the bidding contract to the winning bidder despite the deficiencies in the bidding documents submitted; and
ii. make themselves knowledgeable of the procurement law by attending seminars facilitated by duly accredited trainors and observe strict compliance with the revised IRR of RA No. 9184 regarding conduct of competitive bidding and evaluate thoroughly the completeness and correctness of bid documents submitted by the bidders to ensure that legally, technically and financially capable contractor/supplier is awarded with the contract;
b) BAC Secretariat to –
i. adhere with the posting requirements set under revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process; and
ii. ensure that the Contract Agreement is supported with an SB Resolution authorizing the Municipal Mayor to enter into a contract; and authorizing the LCE to enter into such agreement.
c) Municipal Engineer to ensure compliance with the requirements of relevant DILG MC in the implementation and monitoring of projects funded under LGSF-AM.
observations.managementAction:
Deficiencies in bidding documents were produced and submitted with corresponding justification.
BAC Personalities attended seminars on RA9184 to enhance their knowledge on procurement procedures.
Posting of all procurement items and relevant dates were done in accordance with RA 9184.
Contract of Agreement for infrastructure projects were submitted to SB and relevant authorizations were made.
Compliance with the requirements of DILG MC were duly observed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the MDRRMO to ensure continuous optimal utilization of the LDRRMF through the full implementation of the projects and activities in accordance with RA No. 10121 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.
observations.managementAction:
A periodic evaluation of programs and activities to ensure responsiveness to the needs of the constituents were employed.
The Municipality registered a utilization rate of 73.63% in CY 2022.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed, through its GFPS, to
a) submit the GPB to the Audit Team within the prescribed period for proper evaluation thereof; 
b) consider reconstituting the GFPS to include a specific representative from the PNP’s Women’s Desk, PWDs, private sector, academe, NGOs and women associations; and
c) ensure that the PPAs are aligned with the GAD-related undertakings in order to truly address gender issues towards gender equality and women’s empowerment.
observations.managementAction:
a) GAD Performance Budget were duly communicated to the Audit Team.
Approved GPB for CY 2022 was transmitted to the Audit Team only on February 15, 2023 upon our request.
Reiterated in Part II of AAR.

b) GFPS had been reconstituted thru E.O. no. 2, s. 2022. EO No. 48 s. of 2022 on the reorganization of the GFPS Execom and TWG. However, names of the representatives from Kabalikat – SCO-Women Sector, PNP Women’s Desk, PWDs, NGO, DepEd District Supervisor, and DAPSA President were still not specified.
Reiterated in Part II of AAR.

c) Gender issues were addressed accordingly through various GAD programs per DILG guidelines.
Partially Implemented. GAD PPAs costing ₱21,829,501.45 could not clearly address the gender-related issues and concerns of the LGU. Reiterated in Part II of AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipality continue their goal of obtaining herd immunity to ensure safety of its constituents. We also recommended that Municipality (a) refrain from using cash advance as its mode of payment for procurement of COVID-19 Ancillary Supplies or Services unless authorized by law; and (b) follow the provisions set by the DOH and the NTF in the procurement of COVID-19 Vaccines and its ancillary supplies and services.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor thru the Municipal Treasurer instruct the –
Accountable Officers to –
maintain separate cashbooks for each kind of cash advances, and regularly reconcile their accountabilities with the accounting records; and
coordinate with the recipients, taking into consideration the existing constraints brought about by the pandemic, to facilitate the liquidation of the cash advances within the prescribed period depending upon the nature and purpose of the particular cash advance or as soon as the purpose of the cash advance has been served; and
Municipal Accountant to record the grant and liquidation of cash advances in the proper accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Petty Cash Fund Custodian to replenish the PCF as soon as the disbursement reached 75% of the fund or as the need arises, to ensure availability/sufficiency of funds for petty operating expenses at any given time.

In this regard, we recommended and Management agreed to –
justify in writing and submit necessary proof evidencing that the procurement of medical supplies for use of DRRMO is a proper charge to LGSF-DRRAP, otherwise, effect the reimbursement from the General Fund to the LGSF-DRRAP maintained under the Trust Liabilities amounting to ₱99,900.00 since it is more proper to charge such disbursement in the General Fund;
strictly implement projects in accordance with the priority programs and projects identified and in accordance with relevant guidelines such as LBC No. 134 accordingly, and to ensure that these are charged to the proper funds provided therefor;
direct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly supported with complete documentation required under COA Circular 2012-001 and other relevant rules and regulations, before making any payment; and
effect the necessary adjustment to correct the balance of the Trust Liabilities account; and
instruct the Local Finance Committee to comply with the quarterly preparation of reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation and post the same in compliance to Full Disclosure Policy of the Department of the Interior and Local Government (DILG).

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the –
Municipal Engineering Office to coordinate with the DENR and PENELCO in order to facilitate the installation of the LED streetlights as specified in the contract;
MPDO in coordination with the ICO-MEO to (i) perform detailed planning of the PPAs under the 20% Development Fund to ensure the feasibility of the projects; and (ii) monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and
Bids and Awards Committee and the Implementing Offices to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the –
MDRRMO to ensure continuous optimal utilization of the LDRRMF through the full implementation of the projects and activities in accordance with RA No. 10121 to build a disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks;
Municipal Accountant to ensure the accuracy of the report on utilization of the 5% LDRRMF by reporting only those actually disbursed during the year as “utilized”; and
Municipal Budget Officer to ensure the existence of appropriations for future disbursements to be charged under the LDRRMF prior to certifying in the Certification on Appropriations, Funds, and Obligation of Allotment (CAFOA).

We recommended and the Management agreed, through its GFPS, to (a) continue updating the GAD database in order to develop a more gender-responsive PPAs; (b) consider formulating the projects that could be implemented even during the pandemic to ensure the judicious utilization of the GAD budget; (c) submit the GPB to the Audit Team  within the prescribed period for proper evaluation thereof; (d) consider reconstituting the GFPS to include a specific representative from the PNP’s Women’s Desk, PWDs, private sector, academe, NGOs and women associations; (e) ensure the implementation of all GAD PPAs identified  and approved in the GPB; and (f) ensure that the PPAs are aligned with the GAD-related undertakings in order to truly address gender issues towards gender equality and women’s empowerment.

We also recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the ₱15,600.00 under Due to PhilHealth-Trust Fund to Due to NGAs-Trust Fund so that proper presentation of account balances be made.

We recommended and Management agreed that they remain compliant with laws, accounting, budgeting and auditing rules and regulations and the Local Chief Executive continuously take the necessary actions on the settlement of the disallowances.

We recommended that         Management make direct      payments instead of         disbursing through cash     advances in compliance with |the guidelines; and         

We recommended that the     Management instruct the     Municipal Accountant to     check the completeness of    the supporting              documentations before       certifying on the           completeness and propriety  of the transactions.

Due to (i) incomplete provisions of the ordinance; (ii) absence of financial reports; and (iii) unrecorded construction/ rehabilitation costs of the Dinalupihan Civic Center and Bulwagan ng Bayan, viability of the structures’ operations could not be measured objectively, contrary to certain provisions of PD No. 1445 and the NGAS Manual for Local Government Units (LGUs) Volume I, thus depriving the LGU of the expected revenues therefrom.

observations.managementAction: The Management submitted to the Audit Team the revised report on the compliance of Barangay Bonifacio regarding the barangay’s segregation at source and was already revised in this report. Moreover, the SWMB through the MPDC/MENRO replied that the board  will (i) conduct IEC in every barangay on proper waste segregation; (ii) revisit existing Solid Waste Management Program of each barangay to ensure proper implementation; and (iii) advise the contractors and personnel handling solid waste on proper wearing of PPE and already been issued individual PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management make direct payments instead of disbursing through cash advances in compliance with the guidelines; and
We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the supporting documentations before certifying on the completeness and propriety of the transactions.

observations.managementAction: The Aging of Receivables and request for write-off together with the supporting documents are on-going preparation and will be submitted to the Audit Team once completed.

Stock Cards or the equivalent reports were maintained by AOs, however, the RIS was made in lump amounts upon issuances to the corresponding offices.

SLCs were prepared but updating of the records was not made; valuation of the inventory was also based on fair market values.

Inventory count at year-end was made, however, the required semi-annual conduct of count was not made due to the pandemic.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 4

observations.managementAction:
Physical count of PPE was only made in the first-floor offices as of October 2020. No report has been submitted to the Audit Team related to the physical count.

The Committee already identified scrap materials for disposal, however, there were still to be disposed such as unserviceable motor vehicles and other obsolete properties still included in the books.

Property Cards were not updated, however, details of from the Property Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (PARE) were summarized in excel file which serves as their record. Reconciliation with Accounting is not yet made.

Actual disposal was not yet completed in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5

observations.managementAction: In October 2020, the Management submitted letter request for write off for all liability accounts that are aged 5 years and older in October 2020 attaching the Aging of Payables.  The Audit Team replied instructing them to attach the required supporting documents. No further actions were taken by the Management to push through with the request.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Part II of AAR 2020

observations.managementAction: The Management has implemented the timely submission of the contracts for Infrastructure Projects as well as the DOLE appoved CSHP. The format of work suspension and work resumption orders have been improved to include pertinent information. Likewise, the reasons for suspension have been duly supported with weather advisories or other documents to support the validity of the reason for suspension.

Due to challenges brought by pandemic, leniency in the documentations was noted, these were addressed by the agency in the conducted exit conference and provided justification/ explanation.

Equivalent reports were maintained by the GSO, however, the same were not in prescribed format and not submitted to the Audit Team.

The Municipal Accountant has already initiated communicating with the Provincial Accountant for establishing the ownership of the Dinalupihan Civic Center thru coordination with PEO and MEO. 

The Treasury Department is presently reviewing the fixed discount rates as well as establishing the parameter on the free use of these facilities by government agencies, civic organizations and individuals if the objectives of its use is within the intents and purpose embodied in letter (n) sec. 5.H.03 of the Revenue Ordinance no. 2017-01. 

On vendors, no penalty/sanction is defined in the said Ordinance since they are not allowed to enter the venue when there are on-going events. Private guards, PNP and/or marshalls man the entrances to prevent vendors from entering these facilities.

observations.reason: Photos were submitted, however, the noted deficiencies in the previous documentation was still observed.
Reiterated in Part II CY 2020 AAR

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and the Management committed to take action that the Municipal Accountant -

(b) request for authority to write-off the uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit duly supported with documents required under the Circular.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to- 

(a) retrieve pertinent documents to support the return of unutilized funds to source agencies; 

(b) initiate the reconciliation of the unliquidated fund transfers with the source entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and effect liquidation of balances; and 

(c) request for write-off of unliquidated/dormant fund transfers in the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required in the Circular.

We commended Management through the Accounting Department for establishing mechanisms in monitoring financial assistance and ensuring commitments from the donees/grantees on the completion of the funded projects and activities are in accordance with Section 2 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
	
We also recommended and the Management agreed to install monitoring and control in the operations of the Bahay Pagbabago Treatment Facility to ensure that funds are managed, expended and utilized in accordance with laws and regulations.

We recommended and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant in coordination with other offices involve ensure that procurement of infrastructure projects/civil works are in accordance with pertinent sections of Revised IRR of RA 9184, COA Circular 2012-001 and Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

We also recommended that the Engineering Department prepare and submit PERT/CPM analysis and other required technical documents for every projects implemented by the LGU.

We recommended and the Management agreed that through the Local Development Council, the Sanggunian and the Municipal Budget Officer appropriate and disbursed funds in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated June 30, 2017.

1.12 We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and Management agreed
to instruct the Municipal Accountant to –
a) strictly monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and
validation of the existence of the receivables;
b) prepare aging of dormant receivables on a quarterly basis (Annexes 1-3
provided by the Circular) to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the
remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions; and
c) request for write-off of uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to
the Commission on Audit supported by documents required by the Circular.

3.18 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure compliance and the
functionality of the following responsible committees:
a) Inventory Committee to –
• conduct complete annual physical count of PPE;
• reconcile the actual count and the existing inventory records, if there is any,
and submit complete and signed RPCPPE to the Office of the Auditor not
later than the prescribed deadline by the Manual; and
• identify the reasons for the differences noted before making any
adjustment; and
44
b) Disposal and Appraisal Committee to –
• inspect unserviceable equipment and property to verify justification for
disposal;
• set the final appraised value of all disposable properties;
• recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for approval, the manner
of disposal; and
• conduct public bidding for the sale of disposable property on an ‘as is,
where is basis’ and recommend corresponding award thereof.
3.19 We also reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed
to instruct the-
a) OIC-GSO to (i) cause the preparation and maintenance of complete and
updated Property Cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and
the inventory report; and (ii) take the necessary action to identify all unserviceable
government properties and prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) so that proper disposal could be undertaken; and
b) Municipal Accountant make necessary adjusting entry to record the
derecognition of properties after disposal and correct the balances presented in the
financial statements.

4.20 We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and Management agreed
to (a) retrieve pertinent documents to support the return of unutilized funds to source
agencies; (b) initiate the reconciliation of the unliquidated fund transfers with the
source entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and effect
liquidation of balances; and (c) request for write-off of unliquidated/dormant fund
transfers in the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by
documents required in the Circular.

observations.managementAction:
Accounting Office already keeps updated PPE and Real Property Ledger Cards but not for all the PPE items.

The GSO already set up property cards for the items which have records.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for having a sound environment on disaster preparedness and functional mechanisms in its systems and policies.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the MDRRMC prepare LDRRMFIP and accomplishment report pertaining thereto in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012. Also, management ensure that all claims against governments funds shall be supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMFIP was already prepared and submitted by the MDRRMO.
The Management has enjoined the establishment of MRFs of additional two barangays.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Municipality’s effort to comply with the mechanisms and processes in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women. 

We also reiterate our recommendation to update LGU’s GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines prescribed by the Philippine Commission on Women.
observations.managementAction: Updates on GAD Code are already drafted.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to 

(a) retrieve pertinent documents to support the return of unutilized funds to source agencies; 

(b) initiate the reconciliation of the unliquidated fund transfers with the source entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and effect liquidation of balances; and 

(c) request for write-off of unliquidated/dormant fund transfers in the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required in the Circular.
observations.managementAction: Aging of fund transfers is under-process at the Accounting Office, including retrieval of any available documents to support and explain the nature of each fund transfer. Subsequently, request for write-off of funds that have been dormant for more than ten (10) years is yet to be completed. As for funds that are not yet dormant, coordination are being done with source agencies for the refund of unliquidated balances, hence updated result not yet submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation and the Management committed to take action that the Municipal Accountant (a) strictly monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables; (b) request for authority to write-off the uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit duly supported with documents required under the Circular.
The Management committed to perform necessary procedures to request for the authority to write off from the Commission and to exhaust all the possible procedures to collect other receivables. They committed to comply with the recommendation in the 1st semester of CY 2019.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant to (a) retrieve pertinent documents to support the return of unutilized funds to source agencies; (b) initiate the reconciliation of the unliquidated fund transfers with the source entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and effect liquidation of balances; and (c) request for write-off of unliquidated/dormant fund transfers in the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit  supported by documents required in the Circular.
The Management committed to perform necessary procedures to request for the authority to write off from the Commission, return balances to its source agencies and to exhaust all the possible procedures to locate the agencies/persons with which these accounts were due.

observations.managementAction: Verification and validation of Account Receivables were conducted. Aging of uncollectible accounts are also under-process and being documented for write-off recommendation.

Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) and PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) are now being set-up but not yet updated at the Accounting Office that tallies with the General Ledger balances.

As of May 31, 2018, management already disposed one batch of unserviceable properties. Likewise, the identification of real properties owned by the LGU as recorded by the Assessor is being undertaken. We will dispose unserviceable properties within the year
Physical count and identification of motor vehicles are already completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accountant in coordination with other offices involved, work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Engineering Department prepare and submit PERT/CPM analysis and other required technical documents for every project implemented by the LGU.
observations.reason: PERT-CPM prepared by Engineering Department not yet submitted. Some documentary requirements lacking in some of the procurements of infrastructure projects.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer who oversee the Solid Waste Management Programs continuously educates their constituents thru the Barangay Officials on proper segregation of reusable from compostable waste.                                          The LGU has existing ordinance that contains provisions on compliance by the Barangays on the provision of Republic Act no. 2003. Our Sangguniang Bayan is currently revising major portions of existing ordinances to further strengthen adherence by Barangays on RA 9003. The Local Chief Executive and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer are continuosly doing information dissemination on the operation of Barangay Material Recovery Facility during meetings and seminar update activities. The LGU provided the  barangays with Material Recovery Facility steel frame thru 70-30 share funding.
observations.reason: Deficiencies in documentary requirements were still present in transactions involving Solid Waste Management contrary to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and BUB focal persons ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made in consonance with Section 22 Department Order No. 173-17 Revised Guideline in the Implementation of the DILEEP and Section 3 of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular. 

We also recommend that Management submit the list of recipients for the DILEEP programs.
observations.reason: Absence of monitoring and evaluation of programs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the implementation of the Salary Standardization Law of 2015 in accordance with EO No. 201.
observations.managementAction: 1st tranche was already implemented in 2018, however for CY 2019, 2nd tranche is yet to be implemented. The LGU Local Finance Committee(LFC) is undertaking an extensive study on the financial impact of the SSL 2015 including the PS limitation of the LGU. The LFC will submit appropriate recommendations to the LCE within July 2018.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the commitment of Management in prioritizing concerns of gender equality and women’s empowerment and for implementing last year’s audit recommendation on the creation of the monitoring and evaluation team that will determine if policies, structures, systems and mechanisms were put in place to institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01, or Guidelines on the Localization of the Magna Carta of Women.

We also reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed on the updating of GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines.
observations.reason: Accomplishment report is yet to be submitted. GAD Code is not yet updated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (a) strictly monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables; (b) prepare necessary adjusting entries for transactions affecting receivables; (c) prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a quarterly basis to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions; and (d) request for write-off of uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required by the Circular.
observations.managementAction: Verification and validation of Accounts Receivable were conducted. Aging of uncollectible accounts is also under-process and being documented for possible write-off. Target completion date to implement the recommendation on this finding was October 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (a) retrieve pertinent documents to support the return of unutilized funds to source agencies; (b) initiate the reconciliation of the unliquidated fund transfers with the source entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and effect liquidation of balances; and (c) request for write-off of unliquidated/dormant fund transfers in the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required in the Circular.
observations.managementAction: Aging of fund transfers is under-process at the Accounting Office, including retrieval of any available documents to support and explain the nature of each fund transfer. Subsequently, request for write-off of funds that have been dormant for more than ten (10) years will be prepared and forwarded to COA ATL. As for funds that are not yet dormant, coordination is being done with source agencies for the refund of unliquidated balances.
observations.reason: - Retrieval of documents is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, through the MDRRMC, (a) prepare LDRRMFIP and accomplishment report pertaining thereto; (b) prepare and submit monthly report of sources and utilization of MDRRMF; and (c) include staff responsible for administration and training, research and planning, operations and warning of MDRRMC in accordance with RA No. 10121 and its IRR and COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: A monthly report on the utilization of LDRRMF were prepared by the Accounting Office and submitted to the Auditors. However, these were just based on financial transactions as monitored and recorded by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant (a) strictly monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables; (b) prepare necessary adjusting entries for transactions affecting receivables; (c) prepare aging of dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a quarterly basis to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions; and (d) request for write-off of uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required by the Circular.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed to  (a) retrieve the required documents and coordinate with the previous owners of the motor vehicles for the transfer of the registration; (b) prepare necessary documents for the transfer of donated vehicles; (c) prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) to undertake proper disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles; and (d) initiate investigation to determine accountable officials and employees responsible for the missing equipment and file appropriate charges, if warranted.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the lending institutions for the reconciliation of the loan balances and effect the appropriate adjustments. We also recommend that Management secure certification from the concerned institution of the details of the accounts as support for the necessary adjustments. Henceforth, perform periodic reconciliation of balances of the Loans Payable-Domestic with respective lending banks/institutions to reflect accurate balances.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed to (a) retrieve pertinent documents to support the return of unutilized funds to source agencies; (b) initiate the reconciliation of the unliquidated fund transfers with the source entities, prepare the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and effect liquidation of balances; and (c) request for write-off of unliquidated/dormant fund transfers in the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required in the Circular.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Accountant in coordination with other offices involved, work on the completion of the documentary deficiencies and ensure that the required documents for each type of transaction are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment.

We also recommended and the Management agreed that the Engineering Department prepare and submit PERT/CPM analysis and other required technical documents for every project implemented by the LGU.

We recommended and the Management agreed that (a) through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of barangay officials to educate their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the component barangays be fully functional; (c) assign an a capable personnel to validate the services provided by Metro Clark to support the claim and prepare the inspection and acceptance report; (d) ensure that all transactions are supported with complete documentation in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; (e) Refund the overpayment made on December 13, 2017 amounting to ₱69,000.00 or an equivalent to 57.50 tons and (f) continue their efforts and initiative to include operating a Sanitary Landfill in the long term plan.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal Accountant and BUB focal persons ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made in consonance with  Section 22 Department Order No. 173-17 Revised Guideline in the Implementation of the DILEEP and Section 3 of DBM-DILG-DSWD-NAPC Joint Memorandum Circular. We also recommend that Management submit the list of recipients for the DILEEP programs.

We recommendedand the Management agreed that to make all payments by checks directly to suppliers/labor group leader or laborers through a payroll system.  Payment on reimbursement basis should be made only in exceptional and emergency cases subject to the same rules and regulations and documentary requirements applicable to all financial transactions in accordance with COA Circular 97-002.
We recommended and Management agreed to prioritize the implementation of the Salary Standardization Law of 2015 in accordance with EO No. 201.
We also reiterated our recommendation and the Management agreed on the updating of GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the Management comply with laws, accounting, budgeting and auditing rules and regulations and act swiftly on noted deviations in audit to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:       take advantage of the newly installed PATAS and find ways of the possibility to link the system with the Municipal Treasurer Office; require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers to the Municipal Accountant to be used as basis to record RPT/SET Receivables in the books; and require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SL) that will monitor tax collections and delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS.
observations.managementAction: Maximizing the  use of PATAS, the Accounting  office hoped to be linked into the system on a read-only capacity to be able to monitor tax collections and delinquencies vis-a-vis the basis of RPT/SET Receivables provided by MTO. RPT account registry tax payers' index card (TIC) are already encoded and updated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the: Inventory Committee complete the conduct of the annual physical inventory of PPE by description type and  submit a report thereon to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; Municipal Accountant and OIC-GSO cause the preparation and maintenance of complete and updated Real Property Ledger Cards/Property Cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report, and monitor improvement and disposition of each property; Municipal Accountant (i) verify all the parcels of land owned by the municipality in the listings of Land from the Municipal Assessor and to locate possible documents to support recording thereof in the books of accounts; and (ii) make the necessary adjusting entry for properties that were already disposed; and OIC-GSO completely identify unserviceable properties to cause its appraisal and inspection for disposal.
observations.managementAction: Real Property Ledger Cards (RPLC) and PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) are now maintained and updated at the Accounting Office that tallies with the General Ledger balances. However, inventory to ascertain the serviceability, and the subsequent writing-off of some accounts, have yet to be completed by various inventory teams. Likewise is the identification of real properties owned by the LGU as recorded by the Assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management (a) exert effort and coordinate with the previous owners to retrieve the required documents of the motor vehicles for the transfer of the registration; (b) prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); and (c) undertake immediate disposal thereof to prevent further deterioration.
observations.managementAction: Physical count and identification of motor vehicles were already completed. Documentation to ensure that vehicles owned by the LGU will be named after the LGU and registered with a red plate is being undertaken by the OIC-GSO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all payments for procurement of infrastructure projects bear all the required supporting documents such as project logbooks, as built plans and other important documents/engineering requirements and standards pursuant to DPWH DO No. 216 series of 2000, Section 4 of PD No. 1445, Paragraph 3(j) of Annex “A” of the revised IRR of RA No. 9184, DOLE DO No. 13 and COA Circular 2012-001. We further recommended that Management comply with COA Circular No. 2014-003 so that the policy on full disclosure is adopted.
observations.managementAction: Management gave priority to anti-flooding infrastructure projects to ensure safety of both people and properties. Management committed to be meticulous in scrutinizing supporting documents prior to payment of claims to contractors.
observations.reason: -There were still payments made despite insufficiency of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We still recommended that the LCE: through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; through the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), adopt an ordinance pertaining to policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003; take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the component barangays be fully functional; provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining forty two (42) barangays to establish their own MRF; ensure that all transactions are supported with complete documentation and the attached supporting documents should be in agreement with the particular transaction in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and ensure that all contracts executed by the Municipality should encompasses all the essential elements of the contract.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Officer who oversees the Solid Waste Management Programs continuously educates the constituents thru the Barangay Officials on proper segregation of reusable from compostable waste. The LGU has existing ordinance that contains provisions on compliance by the Barangays on RA No. 9003. Our Sangguniang Bayan is currently revising major portions of existing ordinances to further strengthen adherence by Barangays on RA No. 9003. The LGU Provided the Barangays with Materials Recovery Facility steel frame thru 70-30 share funding. The Accounting Department is scrutinizing all transaction documents for compliance with Sec. 4(6) of PD No. 1445. Contracts are being reviewed by Lawyers from the Provincial Government to ensure that essential elements of contracts are followed.
observations.reason: - Segragation at source is not fully complied with. Several Barangay Material Recovery Facility are not yet fully functional.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer and BUB focal persons ensure that all documentary requirements are attached to the disbursement voucher before payment is made in consonance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: We commended Management for the successful implementation of the BUB PPAs for CY 2016. Management appreciates the commendation given by the audit team. Management commits to conduct thorough verification of all supporting documents prior to payment of claims for BUB projects.
observations.reason: - There were still payments made despite insufficiency of supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed that the (a) Municipality of Dinalupihan coordinate with the Provincial Agriculturist and the PENRO Officer for the proper implementation of RA No. 9003 particularly on the prohibition on the open burning of solid wastes like hay stalks, dried leaves and other farm wastes; (b) Municipal Agriculturist encourage farmers to do composting of farm waste to enrich the soil with natural fertilizers; (c) Management conduct proper information dissemination like conducting seminars, production of posters/tarpaulins on the harmful effects to the health of the public of smoke from burning of solid wastes and assert their authority in the implementation of the ordinance prohibiting the burning of hay stalks and other farm wastes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist already coordinated with the National and Provincial Agencies on the proper implementation of RA No. 9003. We also adopted Provincial Resolution number 03, series of 2009 under Municipal Resolution no. 171, series of 2017 barring the open burning of solid wastes. Our Municipal Agriculture Officer and Technicians are currently training our farmers on decomposting and do information dissemination on harmful effects to the health of smoke from burning.
observations.reason: - Rice straw burning is still prevalent in the area especially after harvesting of palay.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Municipality’s continuous efforts in prioritizing pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment in accordance with the provisions of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01. We also recommended the updating of GAD Code in accordance with the guidelines and creation of the monitoring and evaluation team that will determine if policies, structures, systems and mechanisms were put in place to institutionalize the pursuit of gender equality and women’s empowerment.
observations.managementAction: The Gender equality and womens's empowerment are among the priority concerns of the LGU as spearheaded by our Municipal Social Welfare Officer. We created our monitoring and evaluation Team under Executive Order no.  15 series of 2017. At present we are finalizing the updating of the LGU GAD COde for ratification of our Sangguniang Bayan in the FIrst Quarter of 2018. We already finished our Children's Code.
observations.reason: Finalization and updating of the LGU GAD COde for ratification of the Sangguniang Bayan is still on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management provide personnel to help the Treasurer update the RPTAR/Taxpayers’ index cards (TIC) and submit the Certified List of Taxpayers to the Accountant to comply with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS, Volume I. Based on this, the Accountant should take up in the books, the RPT/SET Receivable and maintain the corresponding subsidiary ledgers (SLs) that will monitor tax collections and delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) encoding of the values of properties was already completed but not yet updated.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 1 The amount set up as Real Property Tax Receivable/Special Education Tax Receivable at the beginning of the year was still based on the real property tax due per barangay from the treasury computed on the 2015 assessment roll provided by the assessor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management request for derecognition of uncollectible receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by (a) list of available records and extent of validation made on the accounts; and (b) certification stating the causes for unlocated records and documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant provided Allowance for Doubtful Accounts for the whole amount of ₱150,290.03 to consider non-collectability of these receivables.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 2 The Management did not yet request for the write off of dormant receivable accounts. They were not also able to provide available records to validate the accounts and certification was not given to state the causes of unlocated records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the: inventory committee conduct an annual physical inventory of PPE by description type and submit a report thereon to the Office of the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year to enable reconciliation with the accounting and property records and correction of verified discrepancies; OIC-GSO cause the maintenance of updated property cards to enable reconciliation with the general ledger balances and the inventory report; Accountant verify all the parcels of land owned by the municipality in the listings of Land from the Municipal Assessor, locate all deeds of donations and titles of the land to support the recording of the land account and update the corresponding PPELCs; and Treasurer request annotation of land without certificate of title from the Registry of Deeds pending the process of titling and allocate funds for the transfer of titles.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 3 Partial completion of the actual physical count and the inventory report, absence of individual property cards/real property ledger cards, non-inclusion of parcels of land owned by the Municipality but not yet titled under its name and improbability to reconcile the account balance of property, plant and equipment with the inventory report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) OIC-GSO fast track the required documents needed for the transfer, insurance with the GSIS and registration with the LTO of the subject vehicles to provide the government adequate protection against damage or loss to property; (b) Management cause the appraisal as basis for the Accountant to record the two ambulances in the books.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 4 Motor vehicles owned by the Municipality were not yet transferred in its name and still bear private plates due to non-location of previous owners and retrieval of pertinent documents.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended and the Management agreed to:
(a) take advantage of the newly installed PATAS and find ways of the
possibility to link the system with the Municipal Treasurer Office;
(b) require the Municipal Treasurer to submit the certified list of taxpayers
to the Municipal Accountant to be used as basis to record RPT/SET
Receivables in the books; and
(c) require the Municipal Accountant to maintain the corresponding
subsidiary ledgers (SL) that will monitor tax collections and
delinquencies in compliance with Section 20 of the Manual on the NGAS.

2.10 We recommended and the Management agreed that the Municipal
Accountant (a) strictly monitor and conduct regular and periodic verification,
analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables; (b) prepare necessary
adjusting entries for transactions affecting receivables; (c) prepare aging of
dormant receivables, unliquidated cash advances and fund transfers on a quarterly
basis to support the request for write-off, and indicate in the remarks column the
existence of the applicable conditions; and (d) request for write-off of uncollectible
receivables from the books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by
documents required by the Circular.

4.10 We recommended that the Management (a) exert effort and coordinate with
the previous owners to retrieve the required documents of the motor vehicles for the
transfer of the registration; (b) prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP); and (c) to undertake immediate disposal thereof to
prevent further deterioration.

5.14 We recommended that the Management (a) retrieve pertinent documents to
support the return of unutilized funds to source agencies; (b) initiate the
reconciliation of the unliquidated fund transfers with the source entities, prepare
the adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and effect liquidation of
balances; and (c) request for write-off of unliquidated/dormant fund transfers in the
books of accounts to the Commission on Audit supported by documents required in
the Circular.

6.10 We recommended that Management require the Municipal Engineer to
ensure that all payments for procurement of infrastructure projects bear all the
required supporting documents such as project logbooks, as built plans and other
important documents/engineering requirements and standards pursuant to DPWH
DO No. 216 series of 2000, Section 4 of PD No. 1445, Paragraph 3(j) of Annex “A” of
the revised IRR of RA No. 9184, DOLE DO No. 13 and COA Circular 2012-001.
6.11 We further recommended that Management comply with COA Circular No.
2014-003 so that the policy on full disclosure is adopted.

7.12 We still recommended that the LCE:
a. through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active
participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management
program of the government by educating their respective constituents on
the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from
compostable wastes;
b. through the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), adopt an ordinance pertaining to
policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the
barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003;
c. take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the component
barangays be fully functional;
d. provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining forty two (42)
barangays to establish their own MRF;
e. ensure that all transactions are supported with complete documentation
and the attached supporting documents should be in agreement with the
particular transaction in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and
f. ensure that all contracts executed by the Municipality should encompasses
all the essential elements of the contract.

10.4 We recommended that Management, through the MDRRMC, (a) prepare
LDRRMFIP and accomplishment report pertaining thereto; (b) prepare and submit
monthly report of sources and utilization of MDRRMF; and (c) include staff
responsible for administration and training, research and planning, operations and
warning of MDRRMC in accordance with RA No. 10121 and its IRR and COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

13.9 We recommended and the Management agreed that the:
a. Municipal Human Resource Officer and the General Services Officer
consider the necessity of hiring additional traffic personnel to monitor the
operations of the three-wheeled public conveyances especially the
violators;
b. revision of rules on the Traffic Citation Tickets be prioritized so that every
incident of apprehension will be recorded and the violators should be
issued TCT tickets to strengthen enforcement of laws; and
c. members of the SB consider enacting/amending appropriate ordinance on
the regulation of the operations of three-wheeled public conveyance to
prevent undesirable outcomes as a result of unregulated operations
thereof.

20.4 We recommended that Management comply with laws, accounting,
budgeting and auditing rules and regulations and act swiftly on noted deviations in
audit to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances.

We reiterated prior year’s recommendations that the (a) the Municipal Accountant scrutinize the schedules of PPE to determine the assets not held or used by the Municipality and make the necessary adjustments; and (b) OIC-GSO expedite the result of validation of the status of the structures and infrastructures recorded in the books of the municipality but were being used by other local and national agencies to determine whether to transfer the assets to the end users or to re-classify them as Other PPE for eventual dropping from the books of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office has already started preparing the necessary adjustments on the transfer of several infrastructure projects to entity/accountable officers.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year's recommendation that Management expedite the reconciliation of the fund transfer balances from the various NGAs. We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant establish the accuracy of the recorded Due to NGAs account by referring to available past years’ records and if able to establish, re-evaluate the accounts to determine if these needs to be refunded to the concerned NGAs or other course of action be implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant already reconciled the given balances per confirmation and coordinated with the source agencies for confirmation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 5 The unutilized balances of Due to NGAs account are still in the books and not yet returned to the corresponding source agencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the SB (a) set a clear policy and formulate implementing guidelines to serve as a basis on the implementation of LGU’s various assistance programs; and (b) revisit/review the resolution for the financial assistance.
observations.managementAction: Updated guidelines in the implementation of the financial assistance to indigent clients was submitted by the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO). The amounts granted for the AICS were already in accordance with the resolution submitted. Also, for CY 2016, Management had already corrected prior year’s observation of recording financial assistance under Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expense account instead of Donations account.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 8 Absence of policies on granting of financial assistance given to other LGUs, organizations and associations, some of the assistance provided to people’s and non-governmental organizations Moreover, appropriate basis for the policy given to employees was not yet prioritized by the SB.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) submit the certification required in the Administrative Order and comply strictly with the policies and procedures prescribed; and (b) direct the BAC to faithfully adhere to the pertinent provisions of the IRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Some procurements for medicines already are supported with the required certification in the Administrative Order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management fully implement the targeted programs, projects and activities under the 20% Development Fund as presented in the AIP and provide specific programs to be implemented in accordance with the provisions of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM for the benefit of their constituents.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 9 The Municipality complied with the guidelines set in DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011. It registered a utilization rate of 83.42%.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management (a) coordinate with other offices involved for the completion of the documentary deficiencies; (b) prepare Construction and Health Program for infrastructure projects and ensure that all Contractors have DOLE-approved CSHP before the implementation of the projects; and (c) require the Engineering/BAC/Accounting Department to ensure that the required documents for each type of transactions are submitted/attached to the DVs before their processing and payment;
observations.managementAction: There is already coordination with other offices involved for the completion of the documentary deficiencies.
observations.reason: Some requirements in the procurement of infrastructure projects were still not met.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated prior year’s recommendation that the LCE: (a) through the Solid Waste Management Board, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (b) through the Sangguniang Bayan (SB), adopt an ordinance pertaining to policies and possible measures that will ensure full compliance by the barangays on the provisions of RA No. 9003; (c) take appropriate action to ensure that the existing MRF of the component barangays be fully functional; (d) provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining forty two (42) barangays to establish their own MRF; (e) ensure that all transactions are supported with complete documentation and the attached supporting documents should be in agreement with the particular transaction in accordance with Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445; and (f) ensure that all contracts executed by the Municipality should encompasses all the essential elements of the contract.
observations.managementAction: The audit team inspected the actual site of the agency’s MRF. We are impressed in the way management handle the operations of the MRF. Site inspected is clean and garbage bins are well-placed. There are separate places for the garbage that were not yet segregated and for segregated garbage such as biodegradable wastes (i.e. coconut shells and leaves and others) and non-biodegradable wastes (i. e. plastic and glass bottles, worn tires and broken plastic parts of motorcycles).
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 7 The remaining forty two (42) barangays is yet establish their own MRF. The Audit Team noted that not all barangays complied with the "no segregation, no collection" policy. According to the staff member of the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO), his estimate is only 40% of households segregate wastes.
